content.full_text:
Re:
Farn PorrucAt- PRACTTcES CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916)322-s6@ o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 10,20M
Victor Quiroz
Friends of Victor Quiroz
l5509Iris Drive
Fontana, CA92335
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-04-112
DearMr. Quiroz:
This letter is in response to yourroquest on behalf of Friends of Victor Quiroz
for advice regarding the campaignprovisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,).|
QUESTTONS
l. May you hold a fundraiser using Active Leadership Motivating Advancement
("ALMA'), a not yet established 501(c)(3) association which will be used to recognize
members of the community, where the profits of that fundraiserwill be used to fund your
campaign?
2- If so, does ALMA have to be an official entity to offer awards and
recognition?
CONCLUSIONS
l. and 2. No, you may not hold a campaign fundraiser in the name ofALMA, as
discussed below.
FACTS
You are requesting advice on the use ofActive Leadership Motivating
Advancement ('ALMA") as an avenue to do some fundraising to help your campaign
committee, Friends of Victor Quiroz, build financial resources toward your campaign for
Fontana City Council.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, ofthe California Code of Regulations.
,gE


File No. A-04-lt2
Page No. 2
You advise that ALMA is not currently structured and that it does not have a
board of directors nor officers, but it is intended to be established as an entity which will
recogruze and encourage active involvement and volunteerism in the community of
Fontana. ALMA does not exist today- You indicate that all expenses incurred to present
the ALMA awards, would be financed by Friends of Victor Quiroz, which is the official
committee to fundraise money for your election to Fontana city council.
You intend to apply officially to make ALMA an official association and structure
a board of directors.
ANALYSIS
A contribution is apayment forpolitical purposes forwhich frrll and adequate
consideration is not made- (Regulation 18215(a).) A payment is "forpolitical purposes"
if it is received by or made at the behest of (among others) a candidate or the candidate's
controlled committee. (Regulation 18215(aX2).) A conhibution is "made at the behest"
of a candidate or the candidate's controlld committee when it is made under the control
or at the direction o{, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with or at the
request or suggestion of the candidate. (Regulation18225.7.)
You indicate that you intend to apply to establish ALMA as an official
association. Under the Act, anyperson2 who receives contributions totaling $1,000 or
more in a calendar year is a "comrnittee." (Section S2013(a).) Thus, if ALMA receives
contributions of $1,000 or more in a calendar year, it is a cornrnitte within themeaning
of the Act-
Section 82A16 defines "controlled committee" as follows:
"'Controlled committee'means a committee that is controlled
directly or indirectly by a candidate or state measure proponent or that acts
jointly with a'candidate, controlled committee, or state measureproponent
in connection with the making of expenditures. A candidate or state
measure proponent controls a committee if he or she, his or her agen! or
any other committee he or she controls has a significant influence on the
actions or decisions ofthe committee-"
Thus, section 82}rc describes two ways in which ALMA may become a
controlled committee- First, by you, as a candidatg exerting significant influence on the
actions or decisions of ALMA and second, by you, as a candidatg acting in concert with
the entity in making campaign expenditures. (Ferguson Advice Letter, No. A-86-044,
copy enclosed.)
2 The Act's definition of "person" is quite broad:
"'Person' means an individual, proprietorship, firm, partnership, joint venture,
syndicate, business trust, company, corporation, limited liability company, association,
committee, and any other organization or group ofpersons acting in concert." (Section
82047.) ALMA, once formed, will be a "person" under the Act.


File No. A-04-ll2
Page No. 3
Generally, a payment received by a candidate is a contribution unless it is clear
from surrounding circumstances that it was received forpersonal purposes unrelated to
his or her candidacy or status as an officeholder. (Section 82015.) Thus, payments to an
organizatron controlled by a candidate could be considered contributions. When
sufficient contributions are received, the organization would qualifu as a committee.
Section 85201requires that all campaign funds raised and spent for a particular
campaign be raised and spent by a single committee established for that campaign. In
addition, it provides that all conhibutions or loans made to a candidatg or the candidate's
controlled committee, shall be deposited in a single campaign bank acoount. This section
is known as the "one-bank-account" rule- The Commission has interpreted this to mean
that a candidate for elective office may have only one campaign bank account and one
controlled committee for each campaign. (Regulation 1852|, Riddle Advice Letter, No.
A-88409, copy enclosed.)
Consequently, a candidate maynot control a campaign committee and be a
conholling member of a nonprofit entity consistent with the provisions of the Act if the
non-profit entity is a controlled committee. However, based upon your description of
ALMA's proposed activity, it may be permissible to fund this type of activity with your
campaign funds in the Friends of Victor Quiroz campaign bank account- Expenditures
for purposes you have described would generally be permitted so long as the
expendifures are reasonably related to apolitical, legislativq or governmental purpose
and confer no substantial personal benefit on you. (Sections 89511 and 89512.)
Nonprofit Organization as a Conholled Committee
Regulation 18217 (copy enclosed) provides that a nonprofit association will be
considered a contnolled committee underthe following cirqmstances:
*(a) A nonprofit organizatiorq as defined in zuMivision (0, shall be
considered a controlled committeg ifboth of the following apply:
(1) A candidatg his orher age,nt, or any committee he or she controls,
exercises significant influence over the actions and decisions of the organization,
or acts jointly with the organization in connection with the making of
expenditures.
Q) Tlre organization qualifies as a committee under Governmemt Code
Section 82013(a), and the organization is operated forpolitical prrposes. For
purposes ofthis regulation, an organization is 'operated forpolitical purposes'if
either of the following applies:
(A) The organization receives or expends funds for thepurpose of
influencing or attanpting to influence the action of the voters for or against the
nomination or election of a candidate orthe qualification orpassage of any
measure.


File No. A-04-112
Page No. 4
(B) The organization makes contributions to candidates or their controlled
committees."
Regulation 18217 also provides a presumption that a nonprofit orgarization is not
a controlled committee if certain criteria me met.
As part of your request for advicg you submitted a proposed invitation to the
ALMA awards- The invitation is a solicitation of funds to reserve seating during the
awards ceremony. It appears these funds are being raised for your campaign commiffee.
When soliciting funds for your campaign committee, the solicitation must be clear as to
the purpose of the fund-raiser. In other words, the donors must know that the funds
received me not going to ALMA, but instead are going to be deposited into your
campaign bank account. Written solicitations for contributions must specifically identify
your campaign committee - the campaign committee forwhich the funds are sought, by
name. (Regulation 18523.1.)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
y|-r^/1 ry
By: Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosures
rMje

2- If so, does ALMA have to be an official entity to offer awards and
recognition?

CONCLUSION: l. and 2. No, you may not hold a campaign fundraiser in the name ofALMA, as
discussed below.
